monday.objective: Students will understand the process of photosynthesis and identify its key components

anticipatory_set.original_content: Show time-lapse video of plant growth. Ask: What do plants need to grow? Where does their food come from?

instructional_input.original_content: Direct instruction on photosynthesis: equation, reactants, products. Use diagram to show chloroplasts and light absorption. Explain energy conversion from light to chemical energy.

guided_practice.original_content: Label diagram of photosynthesis process as a class. Identify inputs (CO2, water, light) and outputs (glucose, oxygen). Discuss real-world applications.

independent_practice.original_content: Complete photosynthesis worksheet: label diagram, write equation, answer comprehension questions. Create visual representation of the process.

assessment.original_content: Exit ticket: Draw and label the photosynthesis process. Write the chemical equation.

homework.original_content: Read textbook pages 124-127. Answer questions 1-8. Bring a leaf for tomorrow's lab.

wednesday.objective: Students will conduct an experiment to demonstrate photosynthesis

anticipatory_set.original_content: Review photosynthesis equation. Preview lab safety rules. Discuss hypothesis: What will happen to plants in light vs. dark?

instructional_input.original_content: Demonstrate lab procedure: set up two identical plants, one in light, one in dark. Explain variables (independent, dependent, controlled). Model data collection.

guided_practice.original_content: Groups set up experiments together. Teacher circulates to ensure proper setup. Students record initial observations and predictions.

independent_practice.original_content: Groups complete lab setup, take initial measurements, create data tables. Begin observation log. Plan daily check-in schedule.

assessment.original_content: Check lab setup and data tables. Verify understanding of variables and procedure. Review safety compliance.

homework.original_content: Write hypothesis and prediction for lab results. Research: Why are leaves green?
